questions.question: What are the two primary composite materials used in the DA42-VI airframe?

options.text: Aluminum alloy and titanium
Kevlar and fiberglass
CFRP (Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer) and GFRP (Glass Fiber Reinforced Plastic)
Steel and carbon fiber

questions.explanation: The DA42-VI uses CFRP for high-strength structural areas like wing spars, and GFRP for lighter-duty surfaces like control surfaces, fairings, and empennage shells.

questions.question: What three major components are housed in the DA42-VI wing center section?

options.text: Fuel tanks, avionics bay, and battery compartment
Two engine nacelles, wing stubs, and fuselage center-section floor
Landing gear wells, hydraulic pumps, and engine mounts
Baggage compartment, fuel lines, and electrical bus

questions.explanation: The wing center section is a critical structural hub that houses the two engine nacelles, the wing stubs (attachment points for the outer wing panels), and the fuselage center-section floor.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the vortex generators on the DA42-VI wings?

options.text: They reduce fuel consumption at cruise speed
They provide lightning strike protection for the wings
They increase maximum cruise speed by reducing drag
They energize the boundary layer to delay flow separation and improve low-speed handling

questions.explanation: Vortex generators are small fins on the inboard leading edge of each wing that energize the boundary layer airflow, delaying flow separation and improving low-speed handling characteristics.

questions.question: Where does lightning energy exit the DA42-VI airframe?

options.text: Through the wingtips via aluminum strips
Through the engine nacelles to the ground
Through a lightning electrode beneath the horizontal stabilizer
Through the landing gear struts

questions.explanation: The lightning protection system conducts energy through a network of aluminum alloy tubes and strips to the rear of the aircraft, where it exits through a lightning electrode located beneath the horizontal stabilizer.

questions.question: Which DA42-VI antenna is not visible during a preflight inspection?

options.text: The COM1 antenna on the fuselage belly
The VOR antenna inside the horizontal stabilizer
The transponder antenna on the vertical stabilizer
The GPS antenna on the fuselage roof

questions.explanation: The VOR antenna is located inside the horizontal stabilizer and is not visible from the outside during a walkaround. All other antennas should be checked for security and damage during preflight.
